creator: fanifos492@dfesc.com
text:
If you install FreeBSD at the end of a storage device (only tested NVMEs, but I don't think it should matter), e.g.

You already have a Windows or Linux install ->

efi_partition
other_os

And then add

freebsd_home /home
freebsd_swap
freebsd_base /

(making sure FreeBSD also shares the EFI partition)

The installation happens correctly, but loader.efi isn't able to find the FreeBSD install to boot of until you specifically point the correct disk at loader.env with rootdev. This bug doesn't happen if FreeBSD is installed first.
